title: Ev Charger Network Expansion Acceleration
thesis: Rapid week-over-week growth in publicly available EV charging locations signals infrastructure investment momentum and policy acceleration, typically preceding EV sales surges. Industrial manufacturing and installation of charging infrastructure drives equipment, construction, and logistics demand.
data_sources: OpenChargeMap API daily charger location counts by state/region
signal_logic: If weekly net new charger locations exceed 200-unit threshold above trailing 4-week average
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly net new charger locations exceed 200-unit threshold above trailing 4-week average Exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if growth falls below 150-unit weekly pace
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use OpenChargeMap API daily charger location counts by state/region via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: EV infrastructure deployment is steady and continuous; weekly threshold breaches occur multiple times per quarter.

data.source: OpenChargeMap API daily charger location counts by state/region

schema.behavior: Rapid week-over-week growth in publicly available EV charging locations signals infrastructure investment momentum and policy acceleration, typically preceding EV sales surges.

market_impact.rationale: Industrial manufacturing and installation of charging infrastructure drives equipment, construction, and logistics demand.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly net new charger locations exceed 200-unit threshold above trailing 4-week average
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if growth falls below 150-unit weekly pace

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: EV infrastructure deployment is steady and continuous; weekly threshold breaches occur multiple times per quarter.

title: Earthquake Aftershock Insurance Claims Spike
thesis: Clusters of moderate earthquakes in populated regions trigger immediate insurance claim filings and property damage estimates, creating short-term sector rotation into insurance and repair stocks. Insurance and reinsurance companies see claim volume spikes; financial sector benefits from short-term repricing of risk premiums.
data_sources: USGS earthquake API daily magnitude 4.0+ events by region with historical comparison
signal_logic: If 3+ magnitude 4.0+ earthquakes occur within 7 days in same region (e.g., California, Pacific Northwest)
entry_exit: Entry: If 3+ magnitude 4.0+ earthquakes occur within 7 days in same region (e.g., California, Pacific Northwest) Exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if no additional earthquakes occur in next 5 days
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use USGS earthquake API daily magnitude 4.0+ events by region with historical comparison via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seismic activity is unpredictable but frequent enough that multi-event clusters occur several times per year in active zones.

data.source: USGS earthquake API daily magnitude 4.0+ events by region with historical comparison

schema.behavior: Clusters of moderate earthquakes in populated regions trigger immediate insurance claim filings and property damage estimates, creating short-term sector rotation into insurance and repair stocks.

market_impact.rationale: Insurance and reinsurance companies see claim volume spikes; financial sector benefits from short-term repricing of risk premiums.

trade_logic.entry: If 3+ magnitude 4.0+ earthquakes occur within 7 days in same region (e.g., California, Pacific Northwest)
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if no additional earthquakes occur in next 5 days

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seismic activity is unpredictable but frequent enough that multi-event clusters occur several times per year in active zones.

title: Weather-driven Utility Demand Spike
thesis: Extreme heat or cold days significantly above/below seasonal average drive peak electricity and natural gas demand, improving utility operating margins and signaling commodity pressure. Peak demand days increase revenue and earnings potential for utility operators; commodity hedges also improve pricing power.
data_sources: Open-Meteo daily temperature and humidity for major US metros; compare to 10-year seasonal norms
signal_logic: If temperature deviation exceeds +15°F or -15°F from 10-year daily average in 3+ major metros simultaneously
entry_exit: Entry: If temperature deviation exceeds +15°F or -15°F from 10-year daily average in 3+ major metros simultaneously Exit: Exit after 5 trading days or when temperatures normalize within ±5°F of seasonal average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Open-Meteo daily temperature and humidity for major US metros; compare to 10-year seasonal norms via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal temperature extremes occur regularly; winter and summer spike seasons guarantee multiple triggering events per month.

data.source: Open-Meteo daily temperature and humidity for major US metros; compare to 10-year seasonal norms

schema.behavior: Extreme heat or cold days significantly above/below seasonal average drive peak electricity and natural gas demand, improving utility operating margins and signaling commodity pressure.

market_impact.rationale: Peak demand days increase revenue and earnings potential for utility operators; commodity hedges also improve pricing power.

trade_logic.entry: If temperature deviation exceeds +15°F or -15°F from 10-year daily average in 3+ major metros simultaneously
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 5 trading days or when temperatures normalize within ±5°F of seasonal average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal temperature extremes occur regularly; winter and summer spike seasons guarantee multiple triggering events per month.

title: Fred Unemployment Rate Surprise Downside
thesis: When actual unemployment releases beat consensus expectations by more than 0.2%, it signals stronger labor demand and consumer cash flow, driving discretionary spending optimism. Better-than-expected jobs data lowers recession fears and supports consumer confidence, directly benefiting discretionary retailers and services.
data_sources: FRED series UNRATE (U.S. unemployment rate) weekly; compare to consensus forecast and prior month
signal_logic: If reported UNRATE is ≥0.2% lower than consensus forecast and prior month reading
entry_exit: Entry: If reported UNRATE is ≥0.2% lower than consensus forecast and prior month reading Exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if next week's jobless claims spike above 5-week average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series UNRATE (U.S. unemployment rate) weekly; compare to consensus forecast and prior month via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Monthly employment reports provide weekly trigger windows; surprise beats happen 3–4 times per year.

data.source: FRED series UNRATE (U.S. unemployment rate) weekly; compare to consensus forecast and prior month

schema.behavior: When actual unemployment releases beat consensus expectations by more than 0.2%, it signals stronger labor demand and consumer cash flow, driving discretionary spending optimism.

market_impact.rationale: Better-than-expected jobs data lowers recession fears and supports consumer confidence, directly benefiting discretionary retailers and services.

trade_logic.entry: If reported UNRATE is ≥0.2% lower than consensus forecast and prior month reading
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if next week's jobless claims spike above 5-week average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Monthly employment reports provide weekly trigger windows; surprise beats happen 3–4 times per year.

title: Google Trends Surge In Luxury Good Searches
thesis: Spikes in luxury good search interest often precede discretionary spending waves and signal consumer confidence inflection, especially around holiday seasons and bonus periods. Luxury search spikes correlate with consumer optimism and willingness to spend on premium goods, directly driving discretionary sector valuations.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for keywords: 'luxury watches', 'designer handbags', 'premium jewelry' vs. baseline
signal_logic: If luxury goods search index exceeds 70 (on 0-100 scale) and rises >15 points week-over-week
entry_exit: Entry: If luxury goods search index exceeds 70 (on 0-100 scale) and rises >15 points week-over-week Exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if index drops below 55
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for keywords: 'luxury watches', 'designer handbags', 'premium jewelry' vs. baseline via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Luxury search cycles align with retail calendars; Q4 and bonus season trigger multiple spikes annually.

schema.idea: Google Trends Surge in Luxury Good Searches

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for keywords: 'luxury watches', 'designer handbags', 'premium jewelry' vs. baseline

schema.behavior: Spikes in luxury good search interest often precede discretionary spending waves and signal consumer confidence inflection, especially around holiday seasons and bonus periods.

market_impact.rationale: Luxury search spikes correlate with consumer optimism and willingness to spend on premium goods, directly driving discretionary sector valuations.

trade_logic.entry: If luxury goods search index exceeds 70 (on 0-100 scale) and rises >15 points week-over-week
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or if index drops below 55

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Luxury search cycles align with retail calendars; Q4 and bonus season trigger multiple spikes annually.

title: Rss News Volume Spike On Food Supply Chain Disruptions
thesis: Spikes in food supply chain disruption news coverage signal pricing pressure and logistics cost inflation, typically benefiting transportation and energy stocks while pressuring consumer staples margins. Food supply disruptions compress staples margins and force price increases that reduce consumer purchasing power.
data_sources: RSS feed count aggregation for keywords: 'food supply', 'crop failure', 'transportation delay' from logistics/agriculture news feeds; weekly volume comparison
signal_logic: If weekly RSS article count for supply chain keywords exceeds 50-article threshold and is >30% above 4-week average
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly RSS article count for supply chain keywords exceeds 50-article threshold and is >30% above 4-week average Exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if article volume returns below 35-article weekly run rate
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use RSS feed count aggregation for keywords: 'food supply', 'crop failure', 'transportation delay' from logistics/agriculture news feeds; weekly volume comparison via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Agricultural and logistics disruptions occur seasonally; weather, pest outbreaks, and transport strikes trigger multiple events per quarter.

schema.idea: RSS News Volume Spike on Food Supply Chain Disruptions

data.source: RSS feed count aggregation for keywords: 'food supply', 'crop failure', 'transportation delay' from logistics/agriculture news feeds; weekly volume comparison

schema.behavior: Spikes in food supply chain disruption news coverage signal pricing pressure and logistics cost inflation, typically benefiting transportation and energy stocks while pressuring consumer staples margins.

market_impact.rationale: Food supply disruptions compress staples margins and force price increases that reduce consumer purchasing power.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly RSS article count for supply chain keywords exceeds 50-article threshold and is >30% above 4-week average
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 8 trading days or if article volume returns below 35-article weekly run rate

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Agricultural and logistics disruptions occur seasonally; weather, pest outbreaks, and transport strikes trigger multiple events per quarter.

title: Tech Sector Volatility Crush Reversal Play
thesis: When tech sector volatility falls to 30% below its 100-day average, it often signals complacency that precedes a sharp volatility re-expansion and repricing event. Low volatility in tech often precedes correction periods as valuations become stretched and sentiment turns; positioning unwinds create sharp drawdowns.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance daily close prices and intraday range for XLK (technology ETF); calculate 20-day realized volatility vs. 100-day baseline
signal_logic: If XLK 20-day realized volatility drops below 70% of 100-day baseline and closes near daily high
entry_exit: Entry: If XLK 20-day realized volatility drops below 70% of 100-day baseline and closes near daily high Exit: Exit after 10 trading days or once volatility spikes above 100-day baseline
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance daily close prices and intraday range for XLK (technology ETF); calculate 20-day realized volatility vs. 100-day baseline via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Tech volatility cycles are frequent and predictable; complacency periods occur several times per quarter.

schema.idea: Tech Sector Volatility Crush Reversal Play

data.source: Yahoo Finance daily close prices and intraday range for XLK (technology ETF); calculate 20-day realized volatility vs. 100-day baseline

schema.behavior: When tech sector volatility falls to 30% below its 100-day average, it often signals complacency that precedes a sharp volatility re-expansion and repricing event.

market_impact.rationale: Low volatility in tech often precedes correction periods as valuations become stretched and sentiment turns; positioning unwinds create sharp drawdowns.

trade_logic.entry: If XLK 20-day realized volatility drops below 70% of 100-day baseline and closes near daily high
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 10 trading days or once volatility spikes above 100-day baseline

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Tech volatility cycles are frequent and predictable; complacency periods occur several times per quarter.

title: Bank Equity Vs Bond Spread Inversion Signal
thesis: When bank stocks dramatically outperform long-duration bonds while yield curve steepens, it signals inflation expectations rising and aggressive rate hike pricing that pressures financial system margins. Rapid bond selloffs combined with bank rallies often precede forced deleveraging as refinancing costs spike and credit spreads widen.
data_sources: Yahoo Finance daily prices for XLF (financials ETF) and TLT (20-year Treasury ETF); calculate relative performance and yield curve proxy
signal_logic: If XLF outperforms TLT by >3% over 5 trading days and TLT closes down >1.5% on the day
entry_exit: Entry: If XLF outperforms TLT by >3% over 5 trading days and TLT closes down >1.5% on the day Exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if performance spread narrows back to <1% over 3-day window
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Yahoo Finance daily prices for XLF (financials ETF) and TLT (20-year Treasury ETF); calculate relative performance and yield curve proxy via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Rate volatility spikes occur regularly; yield curve dynamics shift frequently enough to trigger entry conditions multiple times per month.

schema.idea: Bank Equity vs. Bond Spread Inversion Signal

data.source: Yahoo Finance daily prices for XLF (financials ETF) and TLT (20-year Treasury ETF); calculate relative performance and yield curve proxy

schema.behavior: When bank stocks dramatically outperform long-duration bonds while yield curve steepens, it signals inflation expectations rising and aggressive rate hike pricing that pressures financial system margins.

market_impact.rationale: Rapid bond selloffs combined with bank rallies often precede forced deleveraging as refinancing costs spike and credit spreads widen.

trade_logic.entry: If XLF outperforms TLT by >3% over 5 trading days and TLT closes down >1.5% on the day
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 7 trading days or if performance spread narrows back to <1% over 3-day window

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Rate volatility spikes occur regularly; yield curve dynamics shift frequently enough to trigger entry conditions multiple times per month.

title: Materials Sector Commodity Pressure Washout
thesis: When both crude oil and gold drawdown sharply (>8% over 10 days) simultaneously, it signals macro risk-off sentiment crushing commodities-linked valuations, often creating mean-reversion bounce opportunities in materials stocks. Extreme commodity weakness creates valuation compression in materials and mining stocks, generating technical bounce setups as oversold conditions reverse.
data_sources: FRED series DCOILWTICO (crude oil prices) and GOLDAMND (gold spot) daily; calculate 10-day drawdown from 20-day high
signal_logic: If both crude and gold fall >8% from 20-day highs within 10-day window and XLB closes at 20-day low
entry_exit: Entry: If both crude and gold fall >8% from 20-day highs within 10-day window and XLB closes at 20-day low Exit: Exit after 6 trading days or if either commodity bounces >3% from entry low
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series DCOILWTICO (crude oil prices) and GOLDAMND (gold spot) daily; calculate 10-day drawdown from 20-day high via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Commodity volatility is consistent; major drawdown events occur 3–4 times per year, providing regular entry opportunities.

data.source: FRED series DCOILWTICO (crude oil prices) and GOLDAMND (gold spot) daily; calculate 10-day drawdown from 20-day high

schema.behavior: When both crude oil and gold drawdown sharply (>8% over 10 days) simultaneously, it signals macro risk-off sentiment crushing commodities-linked valuations, often creating mean-reversion bounce opportunities in materials stocks.

market_impact.rationale: Extreme commodity weakness creates valuation compression in materials and mining stocks, generating technical bounce setups as oversold conditions reverse.

trade_logic.entry: If both crude and gold fall >8% from 20-day highs within 10-day window and XLB closes at 20-day low
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 6 trading days or if either commodity bounces >3% from entry low

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Commodity volatility is consistent; major drawdown events occur 3–4 times per year, providing regular entry opportunities.

title: Healthcare Cost Google Trends Acceleration Index
thesis: Sharp spikes in healthcare cost-related searches signal consumer stress and inability to afford medical services, often preceding healthcare system margin compression and policy-driven sector rotation. Rising healthcare cost searches correlate with consumer financial distress, increasing defaults on medical debt and forcing payers to tighten reimbursement rates.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'medical bill debt', 'prescription cost', 'health insurance claim denial'; calculate acceleration week-over-week
signal_logic: If combined search volume index rises >25% week-over-week and exceeds 60 on 0-100 scale
entry_exit: Entry: If combined search volume index rises >25% week-over-week and exceeds 60 on 0-100 scale Exit: Exit after 9 trading days or if search index drops >15% over 2-week period
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly search volume for 'medical bill debt', 'prescription cost', 'health insurance claim denial'; calculate acceleration week-over-week via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Healthcare cost stress is cyclical; earnings seasons and policy announcements trigger spikes 4–6 times annually.

schema.idea: Healthcare Cost Google Trends Acceleration Index

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'medical bill debt', 'prescription cost', 'health insurance claim denial'; calculate acceleration week-over-week

schema.behavior: Sharp spikes in healthcare cost-related searches signal consumer stress and inability to afford medical services, often preceding healthcare system margin compression and policy-driven sector rotation.

market_impact.rationale: Rising healthcare cost searches correlate with consumer financial distress, increasing defaults on medical debt and forcing payers to tighten reimbursement rates.

trade_logic.entry: If combined search volume index rises >25% week-over-week and exceeds 60 on 0-100 scale
trade_logic.exit: Exit after 9 trading days or if search index drops >15% over 2-week period

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Healthcare cost stress is cyclical; earnings seasons and policy announcements trigger spikes 4–6 times annually.
